title: Data Retention Policy
body:
## Purpose
This policy defines the retention periods for records managed by the corporate platform. It ensures compliance with Q2 2027 audit requirements, legal obligations, and operational needs.

## Scope
Applies to all production systems, including transactional databases, logging infrastructure, archival storage, and analytics pipelines.

## Retention Rules
- transaction_log records: 5 years from date of creation, after which they must be securely deleted.
- user_activity_logs: 3 years.
- financial_transactions: 7 years (statutory).
- support_tickets: 4 years after closure.
- access_audit_trails: 6 years.
- email_archives: 7 years.

## Review Cycle
This article is reviewed quarterly by the Compliance Office. Any changes must be proposed via change ticket and approved by the Data Governance Committee.

title: Access Control Standards
body:
## Access Control Policy
All systems must enforce role-based access control (RBAC). Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is required for administrative accounts. Password rotation period is 90 days. Service accounts are exempt from MFA but must use managed identities or certificates.

title: Incident Response Plan
body:
## Incident Classification
- Severity 1: Data breach or critical service outage. Must be declared within 15 minutes.
- Severity 2: Unauthorized access attempt or partial degradation.
- Severity 3: Policy violation without data exposure.

## Escalation Path
Security Operations Center (SOC) -> Incident Commander -> CISO. All incidents must be documented in the ticketing system.
